title: San Util Design: The Tiny F’in UL Nano Pannier
content:
In a move straight out of Bicycle Pubes’s satirical take on the bike industry, our buddy Adam at San Util Design just announced a pre-order for these UL Nano Panniers. San Util makes exceptional bags. John worked with him on panniers for his Ritchey Everest a few years ago, and last year, the FLiP Micro Panniers won bag of the year here on The Radavist. Don’t miss Spencer’s shop visit as well!

It’s great seeing Adam continue to push the possibilities with his products. The UL Nano is a collaboration with Justin McKinley, who, coincidentally, worked on The Radavist redesign many moons ago, came up with a product idea. The UL Nano is a tiny little bolt-on ultra-light pannier that will hold his rain gear and maybe a puffy on the Tour Divide this year. They weigh 140g each and are around 1.5L – 3L in size.

San Util is running a pre-order for these ($95), and we’ve already placed an order to bring a set in for a deeper look.

Sold individually, check out more at San Util Designs!

The post San Util Design: The Tiny F’in UL Nano Pannier appeared first on The Radavist.
author: John Watson
